candidates.title: The Absorption Tax: How Feature Hierarchy Structure Governs SAE Failure Modes Across Semantic Domains
candidates.summary: Iteration 10 focus: fix score regression (7.0->6.5) caused by stale data in paper + add probe degradation ablation. PRIMARY: Cross-domain absorption characterization with descriptive 4x range (11.6%-45.1%), within-RAVEL significance (p=7.4e-66), city-language vs first-letter Bonferroni-significant (p=0.003). SECONDARY: Universal causal mechanism via activation patching (d=0.75-1.50, all p<0.001 -- FULL-mode corrected from buggy pilot d=-0.91). TERTIARY: Decoder direction magnitude analysis (3.98 nats vs 0.12 control, reframed from benign/pathological with acknowledged circularity). NEGATIVE RESULTS: GAS (rho=0.12), CMI (rho=0.044), Absorption Tax quantitative (rho=0.08). NEW: H10 probe degradation ablation to resolve core uncertainty about probe artifact vs genuine hierarchy effect.
hypotheses: H1: Cross-domain absorption rates differ (SUPPORTED: within-RAVEL p=7.4e-66, vs first-letter city-language p_Bonf=0.003)
H2': Semantic >= Syntactic at L24 (SUPPORTED for city-country/city-continent, NOT for city-language)
H3: Hedging decomposition varies by hierarchy (SUPPORTED: strict 0-22.6% vs loose 92.6%)
H4: GAS detector (REFUTED: rho=0.12)
H5: Absorption tax quantitative (NOT SUPPORTED: rho=0.08)
H6: Architecture ranking consistent (INCONCLUSIVE: underpowered, JumpReLU consistently best)
H7: Causal absorption universal (SUPPORTED: d=1.33/1.50/0.75, all p<0.001 FULL-mode corrected)
H8: Decoder direction magnitude (SUPPORTED reframed: 3.98 nats vs 0.12 control, circularity acknowledged)
H9: Rate-distortion predictors (UNTESTED: target rho>0.5)
H10: Probe degradation ablation (NEW, UNTESTED: core experiment for this iteration)
candidates.pilot_focus: COMPLETED extensive. 13+ tasks in iter 9. This iteration: zero-GPU paper fixes + probe degradation ablation (2 GPU-hours) + benign/pathological replication (0.5 GPU-hours) + rate-distortion validation (1 GPU-hour).
candidates.key_novelty: Cross-domain characterization (9/10). Universal causal mechanism (8/10). Tightened hedging classification (8/10). Decoder direction magnitude (7/10 reframed). Negative results: GAS, CMI, Absorption Tax (7/10). Probe degradation ablation methodology (7/10 if confirms genuine hierarchy effect).
candidates.crowded_risks: No competing cross-domain work found as of April 2026. Field active on architectural mitigations (HSAE, masked regularization), not characterization. New: Dissecting Chronos applies SAEs to time series (different domain, not competing).
candidates.compute_estimate: ~3.5 GPU-hours new experiments + ~13 hours wall-clock total (mostly zero-GPU paper fixes).
candidates.falsification_conditions: Probe degradation ablation (H10) shows cross-domain variation is entirely probe-driven AND activation patching corrected data has errors AND hedging decomposition is unreproducible. All three needed to invalidate core claims.
candidates.blocking_dependency: Phase 0 (zero-GPU paper fixes) blocks paper submission. H10 (probe degradation) blocks the strongest version of the cross-domain claim.
candidates.venue_target: Primary: NeurIPS 2026 / ICLR 2027 main. Fallback: EMNLP 2026 / NeurIPS MI Workshop.
key_changes_from_prior_round: H10 (probe degradation ablation) added as highest-priority new experiment -- resolves core probe artifact uncertainty
Phase 0 (zero-GPU critical fixes) added as blocking prerequisite -- addresses score regression 7.0->6.5
Section 5.2 to be corrected with FULL-mode cross-domain patching (d=1.50/0.75 replace buggy d=-0.91)
Benign/pathological reframed as decoder direction magnitude with acknowledged circularity
Headline claims scoped: 4x range qualified as descriptive, 100% pathological scoped to city-continent, no architecture effect reframed as underpowered
Data integrity infrastructure: validate_integration.py + data_manifest.json (10-iteration systemic fix)
Missing figures 4-6 to be generated from existing JSON data
Compute budget reduced to 3.5 GPU-hours (from 10.5) -- project is in paper-completion phase
Only 2/6 perspectives available (pragmatist, empiricist) -- compensated by 9 iterations of accumulated evidence

candidates.title: Controlled Dictionary Experiment: Separating Encoder from Dictionary in Absorption Causation
candidates.summary: Hold decoder dictionary constant, vary encoder (feedforward vs. OMP vs. 2-pass). Tests whether absorption is encoder or dictionary failure. Strengthened by FULL-mode activation patching (d=0.75-1.50) confirming encoder-level competitive exclusion across all hierarchy types.
hypotheses: If amortization gap dominates: OMP absorption < 50% of feedforward at matched L0
If sparsity landscape dominates: absorption similar across encoding methods
2-pass shows intermediate absorption
candidates.pilot_focus: Feedforward vs. OMP comparison on 1000 tokens, Gemma Scope L12-16k. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. First controlled dictionary experiment. MP-SAE does not hold dictionary constant.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if probe degradation ablation (H10) reveals cross-domain variation is entirely a probe artifact, undermining primary contribution.

candidates.title: Feature Absorption as Competitive Exclusion: Phase Transitions and Limiting Similarity
candidates.summary: Formalize absorption through ecological Lotka-Volterra competition and phase-transition theory. STRENGTHENED by: (1) FULL-mode universal mechanism (d=0.75-1.50 across all hierarchies), (2) hierarchy-dependent absorption rates predicted by competition coefficients. Zero additional GPU cost: uses Phase 1 data.
hypotheses: Phase transition: absorption probability sigmoid with transition width < 0.1
Hierarchy-dependent theta*: first-letter < city-language < city-continent (matching absorption ordering)
Lotka-Volterra scaling: theta* ~ sqrt(1/W) * (L0)^(-1/2)
candidates.pilot_focus: Plot absorption probability vs. decoder cosine similarity using Phase 1 data. 15 min CPU.
candidates.key_novelty: 9/10. Zero prior work on competitive exclusion or phase transitions in SAE absorption.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate as supplementary analysis when all Phase 1 writing is complete. Provides theoretical explanation for WHY cross-domain rates differ.

candidates.title: Absorption-Aware Post-Hoc Feature Correction
candidates.summary: Inference-time correction: re-activate absorbed parent features when child fires. All existing mitigations are training-time. Tests whether absorption can be handled post-hoc without architectural changes.
hypotheses: Post-hoc correction recovers >= 50% of SAE-vs-probe gap on downstream tasks
Or: correction has negligible effect because absorption permanently alters decoder geometry
candidates.pilot_focus: Apply correction to first-letter absorption using known absorption patterns. 30 min.
candidates.key_novelty: 8/10. First evaluation of inference-time absorption correction.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers ask for prescriptive guidance (solution, not just characterization).

candidates.title: Within-Hierarchy Variation: Why Europe Absorbs at 90% While Africa Absorbs at 4%
candidates.summary: NEW backup. Depth alternative to breadth. Analyzes per-class absorption variation within a single hierarchy. Why do some parent classes absorb massively while others are nearly immune? Predictable from class size, training frequency, decoder similarity.
hypotheses: Per-class absorption rate predictable from class size + log(frequency) + cos_sim (R^2 > 0.5)
Extreme class imbalance in semantic hierarchies explains why they show higher absorption than uniform first-letter
candidates.pilot_focus: Per-continent absorption rates from existing data. 30 min CPU.
candidates.key_novelty: 7/10. Addresses depth critique from iter-9 review.
candidates.pivot_trigger: Activate if reviewers demand deeper mechanistic analysis within a single hierarchy.

dropped_candidates.title: GAS as Primary Unsupervised Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: REFUTED by full-mode: Spearman rho=0.12 (target 0.6, failure threshold 0.3). AUROC=0.571.

dropped_candidates.title: Information-Theoretic Absorption Taxonomy via CMI
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: NOT SUPPORTED by full-mode: rho=0.044 (p=0.83) at L0=22.

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Tax with Quantitative Predictions (T(G), R_pc)
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Quantitative predictions not supported: absorption-MSE rho=0.08, ranking rho=-0.20, concordance 50%. Retained as qualitative framework only.

dropped_candidates.title: EDA as Universal Weight-Only Absorption Detector
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Works in specific regimes but fails for 75% early absorption. Replaced by GAS, which also failed.

dropped_candidates.title: ITAC as Primary Absorption Detection Method
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Core unsupervised hypothesis refuted by GAS failure (rho=0.12). Conditional independence untested but deprioritized.

dropped_candidates.title: Hierarchy-Coherent SAE Loss
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Crowded by Muchane et al., Luo et al. HSAE, KronSAE. Requires SAE retraining (violates training-free constraint).

dropped_candidates.title: Absorption Scaling Laws
dropped_candidates.reason_dropped: Novelty 5/10. Incorporated into Phase 3 rate-distortion predictor analysis.
